mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings declines, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without monetary suppression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 20-day returns are negative in >50% of triggered observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during post-shock recovery periods, or 5-day forward returns show no positive skew when INDPRO accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day equity returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside expanding industrial production signals synchronized demand and supply chain strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in multivariate regression, or 20-day returns are negative in >50% of synchronized growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion regime indicates economic deceleration and earnings peak risks, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or 60-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of GDP deceleration quarters.
